name: ECDLP verified knowledge graph
purpose: A machine-readable, navigable index of the machine-checked (Lean 4 + Mathlib, no sorry, no axioms) structure of the ECDLP landscape for secp256k1: proved theorems, their import dependencies, the corpus claims they verify, and the formalization barriers that bound them. Built for a future automated reasoner to load the known-and-verified frontier without re-deriving it.
invariant: Every theorem listed is accepted by the Lean kernel with no sorry and no added axioms. Truth is decided only by the kernel; this graph is derived from the ledger, it does not assert proofs.

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_torsion_iff_principal

Obtained by transporting `n`-torsion across Mathlib's AbelÔÇôJacobi map `toClass : W.Point ÔåÆ+ Additive (ClassGroup W.CoordinateRing)` ÔÇö the injective embedding `E Ôå¬ Pic` on which Mathlib *builds* the group law ÔÇö via `toClass_eq_zero` + `map_nsmul`. Key finding: the divisor-class substrate (`toClass`, `FunctionField`, `rootsOfUnity`) is already in Mathlib, so the pairing is more reachable than "from zero" ÔÇö remaining rungs (extract `f_P`, evaluate at divisors, Weil reciprocity, bilinear `eÔéÖÔåÆ╬╝ÔéÖ`) are the open frontier, `notes/FOUNDATIONS.md`)

Extracted from rung 1: `n`-torsion trivializes the class `ClassGroup.mk ((XYIdeal' h)Ôü┐) = (mk (XYIdeal' h))Ôü┐ = 1`, and Mathlib's `ClassGroup.mk_eq_one_iff` turns a trivial class into a principal ideal, whose generator is `f_P`. Proof designed by the Fable model and kernel-verified ÔÇö a piloted "strong-model + Lean-kernel" loop: Fable read the Mathlib source, identified `Submodule.span_singleton_eq_span_singleton`, and produced the proof, which the kernel accepted)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalAt`, `evalAt_surjective`, `evalAt_ker
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing infrastructure ÔÇö point-evaluation homomorphism (`evalAt`: for a rational point `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, evaluation of a regular function at `P` as a ring hom `F[E] ÔåÆ+* F`, built from Mathlib's `quotientXYIdealEquiv` (`F[E]/Ôş¿XÔêÆx,YÔêÆyÔş® Ôëâ F`); `evalAt_surjective` (every value attained ÔÇö `P` is `F`-rational) and `evalAt_ker` (kernel `= Ôş¿XÔêÆx, YÔêÆyÔş®`, the maximal ideal of `P` ÔÇö the functions vanishing at `P`). The first rung of the function-evaluation layer Mathlib lacks (v4.31 has no rational-function evaluation API and `F[E]` is not known to be Dedekind there); regular-function evaluation now exists, the rational extension via localization is next. Unblocks the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)` step)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_isMaximal
theorems.claim: A rational point is a closed point (maximal ideal) (`xyIdeal_isMaximal`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, the ideal `Ôş¿XÔêÆx, YÔêÆyÔş®` of `F[E]` is maximal ÔÇö since the quotient `F[E]/Ôş¿XÔêÆx,YÔêÆyÔş® Ôëâ F` is a field. Establishes each `F`-rational point as a closed point of the affine curve; the prerequisite for localizing at `P` (the local ring `F[E]_P`) to evaluate rational functions regular at `P` ÔÇö the next infrastructure rung toward the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_algebraMap
theorems.claim: evalRatAt` extends `evalAt` (`evalRatAt_algebraMap`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field and a regular function `r Ôêê F[E]`, the value of its image in the local ring `F[E]_P = Localization.AtPrime Ôş¿XÔêÆx,YÔêÆyÔş®` under `evalRatAt` equals its direct value `evalAt h r`. The correctness certificate that rational-function evaluation genuinely extends regular-function evaluation ÔÇö the residue of the localization at `P` coincides *definitionally* with the quotient by `P`'s maximal ideal. Closes the evaluation layer before the Miller function `f_P` is evaluated; proof developed on the warm-server `lake env lean` loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne
theorems.claim: Distinct x-coordinates ÔçÆ distinct closed points (`xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne`: rational points on a Weierstrass curve with `xÔéü Ôëá xÔéé` give different maximal ideals `Ôş¿XÔêÆx, YÔêÆyÔş®` of `F[E]`. Proof: `XClass xÔéü ÔêÆ XClass xÔéé = algebraMap F F[E] (xÔééÔêÆxÔéü)` is a unit when `xÔéü Ôëá xÔéé`; equal ideals would force that unit into a maximal (proper) ideal ÔÇö contradiction. The divisor-support separation the Weil pairing needs ÔÇö the points of `D_Q` chosen to compute `f_P(D_Q)` must avoid `P`, `O`, which requires distinct points to be genuinely distinct closed points; scouted + proved on the warm-server loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_ne` (+ `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne`)
theorems.claim: Distinct points ÔçÆ distinct closed points (full separation) (`xyIdeal_ne_of_ne`: for rational points `(xÔéü,yÔéü) Ôëá (xÔéé,yÔéé)` on a Weierstrass curve the maximal ideals `Ôş¿XÔêÆx, YÔêÆyÔş®` differ ÔÇö combining the x-coordinate case with the `YClass` companion `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne` (`yÔéü Ôëá yÔéé ÔçÆ` distinct ideals, same unit-in-a-maximal-ideal argument on `Y ÔêÆ ┬À`). The complete divisor-support-separation lemma for the Weil pairing: any two distinct rational points are distinct closed points of `F[E]`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff
theorems.claim: Weil W3 ÔÇö zero locus of point-evaluation (`evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff`: a rational function regular at `P` vanishes at `P` iff it lies in the maximal ideal of the local ring `F[E]_P`. From `evalRatAt = residueFieldEquiv Ôêİ residue`: residue-field iso injective, so `evalRatAt r = 0 Ôåö residue r = 0 Ôåö r Ôêê ­Øö¬`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit
theorems.claim: Weil W3 ÔÇö nonvanishing criterion at a point (`evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit`: a rational function regular at `P` is nonzero at `P` iff it is a unit of the local ring `F[E]_P` ÔÇö exactly the condition to evaluate a Miller function `f_P` to a nonzero value in `­Øö¢_p`, off its zero/pole support. Completes the *point-evaluation* half of Weil rung W3; `f_P(D_Q)` over a whole divisor and Weil reciprocity (W4) remain genuine Mathlib gaps ÔÇö see `BARRIERS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ¤êÔéâ root Ôş║ Miller divisor `3┬À([P]ÔêÆ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion`: for a nonzero affine `P=(x,y)` of secp256k1, `(¤ê 3).evalEval x y = 0 Ôåö 3 ÔÇó toClass P = 0` ÔÇö the 3-division polynomial vanishes at `P` iff the AbelÔÇôJacobi class is 3-torsion, i.e. iff `3┬À([P]ÔêÆ[O])` is principal. Welds the division-polynomial torsion tower to Weil divisor-class rung 1 at their shared middle `3ÔÇóP=0`; the first result letting a computable coordinate condition `¤ê 3 = 0` *decide* the divisor-theoretic principality the Miller function needs)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ¤êÔéà root Ôş║ Miller divisor `5┬À([P]ÔêÆ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=5` analogue ÔÇö `(¤ê 5).evalEval x y = 0 Ôåö 5 ÔÇó toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ¤êÔéç root Ôş║ Miller divisor `7┬À([P]ÔêÆ[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=7` analogue ÔÇö `(¤ê 7).evalEval x y = 0 Ôåö 7 ÔÇó toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero` (+ the `Module (ZMod n) ÔåÑÔş¿GÔş®` instance, `secp256k1GÔéÖ`)
theorems.claim: The concrete secp256k1 subgroup `Ôş¿GÔş®` is a `ZMod n`-module (`secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero`: every point of `Ôş¿GÔş® = zmultiples secp256k1G` is annihilated by `n`, so `Ôş¿GÔş®` carries a `Module (ZMod n)` structure built via `AddCommGroup.zmodModule`. This realises the abstract discrete-log model on a genuine elliptic-curve group ÔÇö Mathlib provides no automatic `ZMod n`-module on a cyclic subgroup; it must be constructed from the exponent bound, which the weak keystone `addOrderOf secp256k1G = n` supplies. The bridge that lets every abstract protocol theorem instantiate on real `WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Point` values)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dh_agree`, `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify`, `secp256k1_adaptor_complete
theorems.claim: Deployed signature/agreement protocols are correct on the concrete secp256k1 group (the abstract `Ecdlp.Schnorr` correctness theorems instantiated at the concrete carrier `ÔåÑÔş¿GÔş®` with base point `secp256k1GÔéÖ`, about genuine curve points: DiffieÔÇôHellman agreement `secp256k1_dh_agree`, Schnorr/EdDSA verification `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, Bitcoin Taproot key-tweak `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify` (BIP-341), adaptor-signature completeness `secp256k1_adaptor_complete` (atomic swaps / Lightning PTLCs). Contingent only on `ord(G) = n` + the abstract algebra ÔÇö the honest content: the real secp256k1 protocols verify on the actual curve subgroup, not only an abstract module)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate`, `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify`, `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify
theorems.claim: *(instantiation, multiparty protocols)* the same concrete-`Ôş¿GÔş®` realization for the multi-signer / threshold protocols ÔÇö aggregate Schnorr / Taproot multisig `secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate` (MuSig/FROST), MuSig2 key aggregation `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, Feldman VSS share verification `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify` (DKG), threshold ElGamal partial-decryption combination `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, batch Schnorr verification `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_card`, `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic
theorems.claim: The secp256k1 crypto subgroup `Ôş¿GÔş®` is cyclic of order exactly `n` (`secp256k1_grp_card`: `Nat.card Ôş¿GÔş® = n`, from `Nat.card (zmultiples g) = addOrderOf g` + the weak keystone; `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic`: `IsAddCyclic Ôş¿GÔş®`. The group-object-level statement of "Ôş¿GÔş® is a concrete cyclic group of order `n`" ÔÇö no Hasse/`#E`, only `addOrderOf G = n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dlogEquiv` (+ `secp256k1_dlogHom`, `_injective`, `_surjective`, `secp256k1_gen_addOrderOf`)
theorems.claim: The discrete-log map is a group isomorphism `ZMod n Ôëâ+ Ôş¿GÔş®` (`secp256k1_dlogEquiv`: base-point multiplication `c ÔåĞ c┬ÀG` is an additive-group isomorphism between the scalar ring `ZMod n` and the concrete secp256k1 subgroup `Ôş¿GÔş®` ÔÇö the exact group-theoretic content of the ECDLP setting: the discrete log `dlog = (c ÔåĞ c┬ÀG)Ôü╗` is a well-defined bijection. Its *computational* inversion is the open ECDLP hardness conjecture; this asserts only the unconditional bijection, via injective (`c.val < n = addOrderOf G`) + surjective (`zmultiples`) on the `ZMod n`-module `Ôş¿GÔş®`. Built with `AddEquiv.ofBijective`)
